title: Nur Warsame becomes Australia's first openly gay imam
description: Nur Warsame came out publicly in 2016, making him the first openly gay imam in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Methodist Church elects its first openly gay bishop
description: Karen Oliveto was elected bishop in the United Methodist Church in 2016, the first openly gay person to hold that office in the denomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nicholas Chamberlain is the first Church of England bishop to come out as gay
description: Bishop Nicholas Chamberlain came out in 2016 after a Sunday newspaper threatened to out him, becoming the first Church of England bishop to publicly say he is gay. He stated that he lives with his partner in a celibate relationship, in line with church guidelines that require gay clergy to remain abstinent and unmarried.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nickelodeon's The Loud House shows the channel's first married same-sex couple
description: In 2016 the animated series The Loud House introduced a married same-sex couple, the first on the Nickelodeon channel.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hari Nef is the first openly trans woman on a major British magazine cover
description: One of the special collectors' covers of Elle's September 2016 issue featured Hari Nef, the first time an openly transgender woman appeared on the cover of a major commercial British magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly trans models on a Harper's Bazaar cover
description: Tracey Norman and Geena Rocero appeared on the cover of an edition of Harper's Bazaar in 2016, the first two openly transgender models to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First gay Chilean police officer to enter a civil union
description: Hugo Alcalde entered a civil union in 2016, the first gay police officer in Chile to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First World LGBT Conference for Criminal Justice Professionals
description: The first annual World LGBT Conference for Criminal Justice Professionals took place in August 2016 under the theme "To Connect And Inspire".

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Navy names the USNS Harvey Milk, first ship honoring an openly gay leader
description: The USNS Harvey Milk was officially named at a ceremony in San Francisco on 16 August 2016. It was the first US Navy ship named after an openly gay leader; Milk had served as a Navy diving officer from 1951 to 1955.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile suspends normalization surgery on intersex children
description: In January 2016 Chile's Ministry of Health ordered a halt to medically unnecessary normalization treatments on intersex children, including irreversible surgery, until they are old enough to decide for themselves.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nisha Ayub is the first openly trans recipient of the Women of Courage Award
description: Malaysian activist Nisha Ayub received the International Women of Courage Award in 2016, the first openly transgender woman to be honored with the US State Department award.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Israeli trans woman jailed for refusing military service
description: Aiden Katri, aged 19, was jailed in 2016 for refusing to serve in the Israeli military, the first transgender woman in Israel imprisoned for conscientious objection.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Oregon court grants the first non-binary gender designation in the US
description: On 10 June 2016 an Oregon circuit court ruled that resident Elisa Rae Shupe could obtain a non-binary gender designation. The Transgender Law Center described it as the first ruling of its kind in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US appeals court rules federal law protects transgender students
description: In April 2016 the 4th US Circuit Court of Appeals sided with transgender student Gavin Grimm in his challenge to a Gloucester County School Board restroom policy. It was the first US appeals-court ruling to find that transgender students are covered by federal bans on sex-based discrimination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US military ends discharges of transgender service members
description: On 30 June 2016 the United States announced that qualified service members could no longer be discharged, denied reenlistment, involuntarily separated, or denied continued service for being transgender, effective the same day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel holds its first transgender beauty pageant
description: The Miss Trans Israel pageant took place at a club in Tel Aviv in 2016, the country's first beauty pageant for transgender women.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Two men share the Canadian Navy's ceremonial first kiss
description: In 2016 the ceremonial first kiss between a returning sailor and their partner in the Canadian Navy was shared by two men for the first time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex weddings take place in Cyprus
description: An anonymous couple held the first same-sex wedding in Cyprus in 2016, while Marios Frixou and Fanos Eleftheriades held the country's first public same-sex wedding.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex marriage in a US immigration detention center
description: Tom Swann and Guillermo Hernandez married in 2016, the first same-sex couple to wed inside a United States federal immigration detention center.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Jewish same-sex wedding ceremony in Latin America
description: Victoria Escobar and Romina Charur married in Buenos Aires in 2016 in a ceremony officiated by Rabbi Karina Finkielstein, the first Jewish same-sex wedding held in Latin America.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italian court lets a lesbian couple adopt each other's children
description: In 2016 a lesbian couple in Italy won the right to legally adopt each other's biological children, the first ruling of its kind in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex marriage on the Isle of Man
description: Luke Carine and Zak Tomlinson married in 2016, the first same-sex couple to wed on the Isle of Man.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rallies for and against marriage equality across Mexico
description: In 2016 Mexico saw large public rallies both in support of and in opposition to marriage equality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: 250,000 rally in Taipei for marriage equality
description: In 2016 around 250,000 supporters of same-sex marriage gathered on Ketagalan Boulevard in Taipei, near the presidential office. Less than a week earlier, some 80,000 people had protested against same-sex marriage in Taipei, with a further 90,000 elsewhere in Taiwan.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rare LGBT protest held in Lebanon
description: On 16 May 2016 an LGBT protest took place in Lebanon, a rare public demonstration for queer rights in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Finland
description: Finland's same-sex marriage law came into force in 2017, allowing same-sex couples to marry nationwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in the Faroe Islands
description: Same-sex marriage became legal in the Faroe Islands in 2017, when the legislation extending marriage to same-sex couples took effect in the Danish territory.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect on Ascension Island
description: Same-sex marriage came into force on Ascension Island, a British overseas territory, in 2017.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australia legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Australia passed marriage-equality legislation in 2017 following a national postal survey, and the law took effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Germany legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Germany's parliament approved marriage equality in 2017, and same-sex couples were able to marry from later that year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malta legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Malta passed marriage-equality legislation in 2017, and the law entered into force the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage becomes legal in several UK territories
description: In 2017 same-sex marriage was passed and took effect in Bermuda, Guernsey, the Falkland Islands, Saint Helena and Tristan da Cunha. In Bermuda the right was temporarily repealed between June and November 2018 before being restored.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chiapas, Baja California and Puebla adopt same-sex marriage
description: The Mexican states of Chiapas, Baja California and Puebla legalized same-sex marriage in 2017, with the changes taking effect the same year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Austrian Constitutional Court opens marriage to same-sex couples
description: In 2017 Austria's Constitutional Court ruled that same-sex couples must be allowed to marry, with the ruling to take effect by 1 January 2019.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiwan's top court rules for same-sex marriage
description: Taiwan's Constitutional Court ruled in 2017 that barring same-sex couples from marriage was unconstitutional, requiring marriage equality to take effect by 24 May 2019. The decision put Taiwan on course to become the first jurisdiction in Asia with same-sex marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Alderney passes same-sex marriage legislation
description: The Channel Island of Alderney passed legislation in 2017 to allow same-sex couples to marry.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chad criminalizes homosexuality
description: Chad criminalized same-sex sexual activity in 2017 under its new penal code, making consensual same-sex relations a punishable offense.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada passes Bill C-16 protecting transgender people
description: In 2017 Canada passed Bill C-16, banning discrimination against transgender people federally and treating anti-trans bias as an aggravating factor in hate crimes. New Brunswick, Nunavut and Yukon passed similar protections plus legal gender change without surgery, extending anti-discrimination coverage for trans people to every province and territory.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: India's Supreme Court declares privacy a right protecting sexual orientation
description: In 2017 the Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to privacy is protected under the Constitution and criticized the reasoning of the 2013 Koushal decision that had reinstated Section 377. The ruling placed sexual orientation within the constitutional protection of privacy and opened the door to a stronger challenge against Section 377.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Disney's Beauty and the Beast features its first gay character
description: In the 2017 live-action adaptation of Beauty and the Beast, the character Le Fou is portrayed as gay, a first for a Disney film.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marshall Bang debuts as Korea's first openly gay singer
description: In 2017 Marshall Bang became the first singer in Korea to launch his music career while openly gay.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queer British Art exhibition headlines the Tate's 2017 program
description: The Queer British Art show led the Tate's 2017 exhibition program, presenting a major survey of queer art in Britain.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: National Geographic puts a transgender girl on its cover
description: The January 2017 issue of National Geographic featured nine-year-old Avery Jackson on its cover, thought to be the first openly transgender person on the magazine's cover.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ellen Hart is the first openly LGBT Grand Master of mystery writing
description: In 2017 the Mystery Writers of America named openly lesbian author Ellen Hart a Grand Master, the first openly LGBT writer to receive the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boy Scouts of America admit their first openly transgender member
description: Joe Maldonado joined the Boy Scouts of America in 2017 as its first openly transgender member. He had been rejected from the Cub Scouts in 2016 for being transgender; the organization changed its policy after his case drew national attention.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly trans actress nominated for a Canadian Screen Award
description: Gabrielle Tremblay was nominated for Best Supporting Actress at the 2017 Canadian Screen Awards for Those Who Make Revolution Halfway Only Dig Their Own Graves, the first openly transgender actress to receive such a nomination.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Valentina Sampaio is the first openly trans model on French Vogue's cover
description: Brazilian model Valentina Sampaio appeared on the cover of French Vogue in 2017, the first openly transgender model to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly trans woman presents a trophy at the Grammys
description: Martina Robledo acted as a trophy presenter at the 2017 Grammy Awards, the first openly transgender woman in that role.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Moonlight is the first LGBT film to win the Best Picture Oscar
description: At the 2017 Academy Awards, Moonlight won Best Picture, the first LGBT-themed film to take the top Oscar.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly non-binary deacon commissioned in the United Methodist Church
description: In 2017 M Barclay was commissioned as a deacon in the United Methodist Church, the first openly non-binary trans person to hold the role.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Venice's first openly transgender gondolier
description: Alex Hai came out as a transgender man in 2017, becoming the first openly transgender gondolier in Venice.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ryan Atkin becomes English football's first openly gay match official
description: Referee Ryan Atkin came out in 2017, making him the first openly gay match official in English football.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Katie Sowers becomes the NFL's first openly LGBT coach
description: San Francisco 49ers assistant coach Katie Sowers came out as gay in 2017, the first openly LGBT coach in the NFL and in US men's professional sports.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay player in a top-division US college football game
description: In 2017 Scott Frantz became the first openly gay college football player to appear in a game for an NCAA Division I Football Bowl Subdivision school.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First LGBT-inclusive interfaith gathering in West Africa
description: In August 2017 the Interfaith Diversity Network of West Africa hosted the region's first LGBT-inclusive religious gathering, themed "Building Bridges, Sharing Stories, Creating Hope". More than 30 participants came from ten West African countries, including Nigeria, Ghana, Benin, Sierra Leone, Liberia, The Gambia, Burkina Faso, Ivory Coast, Mali and Togo.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: MOGA releases the first rainbow hijab
description: In 2017 the Muslim women's fashion label MOGA released a rainbow hijab, described as the first by any fashion designer worldwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Texas gets its first known openly transgender elected official
description: Jess Herbst, mayor of New Hope, came out as transgender in 2017, becoming the first known openly transgender elected official in Texas history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
